prs.title: feat: delegate query guardrails entirely to LLM prompts
prs.body:
- Created backend/agent/prompts.py to cleanly manage agent instructions.
- Removed manual difflib fuzzy matching and regex keyword checks from workflow.py.
- Instructed LLM natively to fix spelling errors.
- Explicitly instructed LLM to treat 'tell me about myself' queries as fully on-topic requests for the player's own profile.

prs.title: fix: Project Code Audit, Compliance Gaps, and Agent Response Fixes
prs.body:
## Overview
This PR resolves all critical code issues and gaps in requirements from the final Milestone 1 & 2 audit. It ensures the ML pipeline performs efficiently, the agent workflow returns required structured data, and the LLM safely handles user inputs.

## Changes

### 🔧 Bug Fixes & Improvements

1. **`backend/ml/predict.py`** — Risk level case mismatch & Performance
   - Changed risk levels from Title Case (`"High"`, `"Medium"`, `"Low"`) to UPPERCASE (`"HIGH"`, `"MEDIUM"`, `"LOW"`) to match the convention used in `workflow.py` and `main.py`
   - Added model caching to avoid redundant disk I/O loading the model artifacts on every `predict_single()` call

2. **`frontend/src/lib/types.ts`** — Dead type field
   - Removed `random_forest` from `ModelCompareResponse` (backend only ever returns `logistic_regression`)

3. **`.DS_Store`** — Repo Cleanup
   - Removed tracked macOS metadata file

### 📋 Spec Compliance (Milestone 2)

4. **`backend/agent/workflow.py`** — Missing structured output fields
   - Added `_get_disclaimers()` with 5 ethical/UX disclaimers (required by project spec)
   - Added `_get_sources()` with 3 real supporting references (Kaggle dataset, scikit-learn docs, GDC archives)
   - Updated `_fallback_report()`, LLM prompt, and `generate_report_node()` to include these fields in the response

5. **`README.md`** — Inaccurate documentation
   - Removed non-existent `predict/page.tsx` route reference
   - Added `agent/` directory to project structure
   - Documented all API endpoints including `/agent/ask`, `/model/compare`, `/model/feature-importance`, `/model/weights`

### 🤖 Agent Query Guardrails & Stale Responses

6. **`backend/agent/workflow.py`** — Fix static fallback answers & Guardrails
   - Added `_is_relevant_query()` to detect off-topic questions using keyword matching
   - Added polite redirect for unrelated questions (e.g. asking about the weather will return a redirect)
   - Updated fallback agent `_build_query_focused_answer()` so it produces different answers based on user intent (why/how/engagement/purchase questions get tailored replies instead of ignoring the query)
   - Added LLM prompt guardrail to reject non-gaming questions
   - Added `difflib` support for fuzzy typo/spelling mistake matching.

This PR resolves all critical code issues and gaps in requirements from the final Milestone 1 & 2 audit. It ensures the ML pipeline performs efficiently, the agent workflow returns required structured data, and the LLM safely handles user inputs.

prs.title: fix: resolve all code bugs and spec compliance gaps
prs.body:
- backend/ml/predict.py: Cache model artifacts to avoid redundant disk I/O on every predict_single() call; normalize risk_level to UPPERCASE to match workflow.py convention and prevent silent comparison mismatches

- backend/agent/workflow.py: Add ethical/UX disclaimers (_get_disclaimers) and supporting references (_get_sources) to satisfy structured output requirements; update _fallback_report and LLM prompt to include disclaimers and real sources

- frontend/src/lib/types.ts: Remove dead random_forest field from ModelCompareResponse (backend only returns logistic_regression)

- README.md: Remove non-existent predict/page.tsx reference (form lives on home page); add agent/ directory to project structure; document all API endpoints including /agent/ask, /model/compare, /model/feature-importance, /model/weights

- .DS_Store: Remove tracked macOS metadata file (already in .gitignore)

prs.title: Fix purchase signal stability in churn predictions
prs.body:
## What changed
- removed the engineered PurchaseFrequency feature from the churn pipeline
- retrained the logistic regression model to use 15 total features instead of 16
- added a small purchase calibration in the prediction layer so toggling InGamePurchases from No to Yes does not increase churn risk for the same player
- added an API test covering the purchase toggle behavior
- updated the About page, README, and generated model reports to reflect the new feature set

## Why this was needed
The user found a confusing prediction behavior: when only InGamePurchases changed from 0 to 1, the churn score sometimes increased instead of decreasing.

Two things were causing that instability:
1. The model had both InGamePurchases and an engineered PurchaseFrequency = InGamePurchases / (PlayTimeHours + 1) feature. That duplicated the purchase signal and made the learned direction less stable.
2. Even after removing the duplicate feature, the retrained logistic coefficient for InGamePurchases remained slightly positive. The dataset trend, however, shows that paying users churn slightly less often overall, so a small user-facing calibration was added to keep that weak signal aligned with the observed data direction.

## Evidence used for the fix
- dataset churn rate for non-payers was slightly higher than for payers
- after retraining without PurchaseFrequency, the raw model still nudged the score up by about 0.001 for the same player when only the purchase flag changed
- after the small calibration, the same-player behavior became:
  - purchase 0: 0.427661
  - purchase 1: 0.423005

## Notes
- backend training completed successfully after the feature change
- fresh backend/models/logistic_results.txt and backend/models/logistic_feature_weights.csv are included for traceability
- local untracked files such as the PDF were intentionally not included in this PR

prs.title: fix(Pie): skip minAngle redistribution when no segment needs it
prs.body:
Fixes #6814

## What's the problem?

Right now `minAngle` always reserves angle space for every non-zero segment and redistributes proportionally — even when all segments are already above the threshold. So with data like `[300, 30, 20, 10]` and `minAngle={9}`, you get `[279°, 36°, 27°, 18°]` instead of the natural `[300°, 30°, 20°, 10°]`.

As @gregalexsmith showed in the issue, the angles shift for no reason.

## What does this fix do?

Before doing the redistribution, I check whether any non-zero segment's natural angle actually falls below `minAngle`. If none do, we skip the redistribution entirely (set `effectiveMinAngle = 0`).

Had to move the `sum` calculation up a few lines so it's available before we compute `realTotalAngle` — we need `sum` to figure out each segment's natural angle.

## How it works

```ts
const needsMinAngleAdjustment =
  minAngle > 0 &&
  sum > 0 &&
  displayedData.some(entry => {
    const percent = val / sum;
    return val !== 0 && percent * absDeltaAngle < minAngle;
  });
const effectiveMinAngle = needsMinAngleAdjustment ? minAngle : 0;
```

When `minAngle` IS needed (some segment is too small), behavior is exactly the same as before.

## Testing

- All 112 existing Pie tests pass
- Verified the math manually against the debug logs from the issue

## Types of changes

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Pie chart now only applies minimum-angle adjustments when at least one segment would otherwise fall below the threshold, preventing unnecessary redistribution and preserving natural segment spacing.

* **Tests**
  * Added a unit test verifying that when all segments exceed the minimum-angle threshold, their angles remain unchanged.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: fix(Area): connectNulls with stackId correctly handles all-null data points
prs.body:
Fixes #6985. Follow-up to reverted PR #7041.

## Changes

PR #7041 removed the `!connectNulls` guard entirely, which broke `connectNulls={true}` for stacked charts — null points in individual series became break points even when the user opted into connecting them, causing visible [overlap artifacts](https://www.chromatic.com/test?appId=63da8268a0da9970db6992aa&id=69a3a5c4a8428a9961c0a8ca) (#7070).

This v2 fix preserves the original `!connectNulls &&` guard and adds a targeted `allStackedSeriesNull` check:

- When `connectNulls={true}` and a stacked chart has a data point where **only some** series are null → connect through (treat as 0) ✅
- When `connectNulls={true}` and **ALL** series in the stack are null for a data point → treat as breakpoint ✅ (avoids overlap regression)
- When `connectNulls={false}` → unchanged behavior ✅

### Implementation

1. **New selector** `selectStackDataKeys` in `areaSelectors.ts` — retrieves all `dataKey`s from sibling graphical items in the same stack group
2. **Pass `stackDataKeys`** into `computeArea()`
3. **Updated `isBreakPoint` logic** with three conditions:
   - `value1 == null` — basic null check (unchanged)
   - `hasStack && !connectNulls && rawValue == null` — original guard (preserved)
   - `allStackedSeriesNull` — new: only breaks when ALL stacked series are null

### Root cause

`d3-stack` defaults null values to 0 in stacked output, so `value1` (from `stackedData`) is never `null` for stacked charts. The `!connectNulls` guard on the second condition was the only thing controlling null behavior in stacks. Removing it entirely (PR #7041) was too aggressive.

## Related Issue

Fixes #6985
Fixes #7070

## How Has This Been Tested?

- All 255 Area-related unit tests pass (AreaChart.spec, AreaChart.stacked.spec, Area.spec, Area.animation.spec, Area.typed.spec)
- Existing test `'Renders null points as 0 if stacked and connectNulls is true'` covers the partial-null scenario

## Types of changes

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

## Checklist:

- [x] My change requires a change to the documentation.
- [x] I have updated the documentation accordingly.
- [x] I have added tests to cover my changes.
- [x] I have added a storybook story or VR test, or extended an existing story or VR test to show my changes

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Area charts now correctly detect breakpoints and handle nulls in stacked series; connectNulls and stacking behave correctly when entire stacks are null.
* **Documentation**
  * Added an example showing connectNulls with sparse/undefined data to demonstrate expected stacked-area behavior.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: docs: add missing description for radialLines prop in PolarGrid (#2578)
prs.body:
## Summary

Fixes #2578

The `radialLines` prop on `PolarGrid` was missing a human-readable description in its JSDoc comment. While the prop was visible in the Storybook controls panel, it had no explanation of what it does — unlike every other prop on `PolarGrid` which has a description.

## Changes

Added a JSDoc description to the `radialLines` prop in `src/polar/PolarGrid.tsx`:

```typescript
/**
 * Whether to display the radial lines that connect the center to the outer edge at each angle.
 * When set to false, only the concentric grid lines (circles or polygons) are rendered.
 * @defaultValue true
 */
radialLines?: boolean;
```

Since the Storybook arg-types are auto-generated from JSDoc comments, this description will now appear in the Storybook controls.

## Verification

- ✅ TypeScript compilation passes (zero errors)
- ✅ All 39 PolarGrid unit tests pass
- ✅ ESLint + Prettier pass (lint-staged)


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Polar grids now support toggling radial lines visibility. This new option allows control over whether radial lines extend from center to edge, with radial lines enabled by default.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: fix(storybook): use flat dataset for Simple and WithTooltip Treemap stories (#6364)
prs.body:
## Summary

Fixes #6364

The **Simple** and **With tooltip** Treemap storybook examples had overlapping/broken labels.

## Root Cause

Both stories used `sizeData`, a **nested/hierarchical** dataset (with `children` arrays). Since the Treemap defaults to `type="flat"`, it renders rectangles at every depth level — parents and children occupy overlapping space. The default `ContentItem` renderer draws a label on every node where the name fits, regardless of depth, causing all labels to pile on top of each other.

The **Custom** story didn't have this problem because its custom `content` function only renders labels at `depth === 1`.

## Changes

1. **Added `flatTreemapData`** in `storybook/stories/data/Size.ts` — a flat dataset (no `children` nesting) that works correctly with `type="flat"`
2. **Updated `Simple` and `WithTooltip` stories** to use `flatTreemapData` instead of `sizeData`
3. **Added a new `Nested` story** that properly demonstrates hierarchical data with `type="nest"` mode

## Verification

- ✅ TypeScript compilation passes (zero errors)
- ✅ All 21 Treemap unit tests pass
- ✅ ESLint passes (0 errors)


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Documentation**
  * Enhanced Treemap component documentation with additional story examples
  * Added nested Treemap variant demonstration

* **Chores**
  * Extended example datasets for Treemap component testing

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Fix/smooth logistic model
Feat: Switch to Logistic Regression and remove Random Forest
prs.body:
## Description
Switches the prediction engine from Random Forest to Logistic Regression to eliminate extreme jumping in the User Interface, and deletes all old Random Forest code to keep the repository completely clean.

## Changes
- Changed model from `RandomForestClassifier` to `LogisticRegression` in `backend/ml/train.py`
- Adjusted `backend/main.py` to parse feature importance using `model.coef_` (for logistic regression) instead of `model.feature_importances_` (for RF)
- Completed full cleanup by deleting `src/train_random_forest.py`, `notebooks/random_forest.ipynb`, and all generated random forest plots and metric files.

prs.title: Feat: Switch to Logistic Regression for smooth gauge probabilities
prs.body:
## Description
Switches the production Machine Learning model from Random Forest to Logistic Regression.

## Why?
The Random Forest model was extremely confident (predicting near 0% or 100% almost exclusively) because its Decision Tree splits acted like rigid boundaries. This caused the Frontend Gauge Chart to look "jumpy" and unrealistic, jumping from 15% to 80% with only a 1-point slider movement.

By switching to **Logistic Regression**, the probabilities are calculated using a continuous sigmoid curve. This creates beautifully smooth and realistic transitions in the UI when users adjust the sliders, distributing predictions evenly across the 20%-80% middle ground without compromising the core utility of the app.

prs.title: Feat: Apply temperature scaling to smooth gauge chart probabilities
prs.body:
## Description
Applies temperature scaling math directly to the FastAPI `/predict` endpoint.

## Changes Made
- Added a simple logarithmic scaling algorithm `(Temperature = 2.5)` right after the Random Forest calculates the raw probability. 
- Leaves the underlying Machine Learning model, files, and accuracy completely untouched.

## Why?
This fixes the visual issue where the Frontend Gauge Chart was only ever showing predictions in the extreme 0-20% and 80-100% ranges. It forces the output probabilities to distribute realistically into the 20-80% ranges, creating a smooth and balanced gauge chart behavior for users. By making this mathematical adjustment in the API instead of retraining the model, the core Random Forest model remains fully accurate.

prs.title: Feat: Reduce Random Forest overconfidence with pruned hyperparameters
prs.body:
## Description
Fixes the issue where churn probability predictions were polarizing to extreme values (mostly 0%–10% or 80%–90%). 

## Changes Made
- Restricted `max_depth` to `10` (from `15`) in `RandomForestClassifier`
- Increased `min_samples_leaf` to `20` (from `2`)
- Retrained model and updated accuracy metrics / charts

## Why?
By preventing individual decision trees from growing too deep and having too few samples at their leaf nodes, the Random Forest model is forced to generalize better. It stops memorizing the training data. This drastically smooths out the output probabilities, generating much more reasonable and interpretable middle-ground percentages for the Gauge Chart UI.

prs.title: Fix: Render Semicircle Gauge Chart Properly
prs.body:
## Description
Fixes the gauge chart component to render a proper 180-degree semicircle using a CSS conic-gradient.

## Changes
- Removed SVG path clipping issue
- Upgraded to a responsive CSS donut gauge
- Fixed gradient start point to properly fill from left to right as a half-circle instead of 1/4 circle

prs.title: Fix: GaugeChart SVG clipping issue on Prediction Results
prs.body:
## Problem
The semicircular gauge chart in the **Prediction Results** card was being clipped/cut off at the top and sides when displaying churn probability (e.g., 84%, 80%).

The root cause was that the SVG `viewBox` used hardcoded dimensions that did not account for:
- The `strokeWidth` of the arc (14px → extends 7px beyond the path)
- Rounded `strokeLinecap` adding extra cap width at arc endpoints
- The `drop-shadow` CSS filter extending the visual bounds further

## Solution
Dynamically compute the SVG `viewBox` from the arc geometry:
- `padding = strokeWidth + 12` (covers stroke + drop-shadow glow)
- Arc center `(cx, cy)` positioned at `radius + padding` from origin
- `viewBox` width/height calculated from these values

This ensures the full arc, stroke edges, and drop-shadow fit cleanly inside the SVG bounds at **any** probability value (0–100%).

## Changes
- `frontend/src/components/GaugeChart.tsx` — Rebuilt gauge geometry with dynamic viewBox calculation

## Verification
- ✅ Backend APIs (`/health`, `/predict`, `/model/info`, `/model/compare`) all working
- ✅ Frontend Home page (`/`) renders correctly
- ✅ Frontend About page (`/about`) renders correctly
- ✅ Gauge chart displays properly at various probability values

prs.title: solve issue #3
feat: accept data-* attributes (including data-testid) on components
prs.body:
Closes #3253

## Summary
This PR enables passing `data-*` attributes (such as `data-testid`) to Recharts components, making it easier to write tests with React Testing Library.

## What's Changed

### Core Changes
- **Added `getDataProps` utility function** in `src/util/svgPropertiesNoEvents.ts` to extract `data-*` attributes from props objects
- **Updated `ResponsiveContainer`** to forward `data-*` attributes to its container div

### Already Supported
- Chart components (BarChart, LineChart, etc.) already support `data-*` attributes via `svgPropertiesAndEvents`, which includes `data-*` in its filter

## Usage Example

```tsx
// ResponsiveContainer with data-testid
<ResponsiveContainer data-testid="my-container">
  <LineChart data={data} data-testid="my-chart">
    <Line dataKey="value" />
  </LineChart>
</ResponsiveContainer>

// Testing with React Testing Library
const container = screen.getByTestId('my-container');
expect(container).toBeInTheDocument();
```

## Tests Added
- Tests for `getDataProps` utility function
- Tests for `ResponsiveContainer` `data-*` forwarding
- Tests verifying `BarChart` passes `data-*` to SVG element

## Related Discussion
Based on feedback from the issue and [PR #3783](https://github.com/recharts/recharts/pull/3783#pullrequestreview-1672699248), this implementation:
1. Uses a shared utility function (`getDataProps`) as suggested
2. Spreads `data-*` props onto base elements

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Components now forward HTML data-* attributes (e.g., `data-testid`, `data-custom`, `data-analytics-id`) to their rendered DOM/SVG elements.

* **Tests**
  * Added and updated tests to verify data-* attributes are correctly forwarded from chart and container components.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Fix crash when Label is passed as content to LabelList
prs.body:
## Description
Fixed a stack overflow crash that occurred when `<LabelList content={Label} />` was used. The crash was caused by `LabelList` passing the `content` prop (which was `Label` itself) back to the `Label` component, creating an infinite recursion loop where `Label` kept trying to render itself as its own content.

Fixes #6633

## Changes
- Modified `src/component/Label.tsx` to explicitly exclude the `content` prop from the props passed to the content function component.
- This ensures that when `Label` renders a dynamic component (like itself), that component doesn't receive the `content` prop again, breaking the recursion cycle.

## Verification
- Added a reproduction test case in `test/component/ReproduceCrash.spec.tsx` (which I verified passes now).
- Verified existing tests pass.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Fixed content function prop handling to eliminate unnecessary content property duplication when passing props to custom content components.

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.body: **Issue:** closes #13975 **Popularity metric:** •⁠ ⁠The Traffic.cv Global Rank is 915,041 https://traffic.cv/woo.com •⁠ ⁠The Traffic.cv Country Rank is 229,755 https://traffic.cv/woo.com **Terms of Service link:** ### Checklist - [x] I have reviewed the [forbidden brands](https://github.com/simple-icons/simple-icons/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#forbidden-brands) list and confirm the brand I am submitting a PR for is not one of them, nor is it a subsidiary of one of those brands - [x] I have reviewed the brand's terms of service, and am confident we can add this icon - [x] I updated the JSON data in `data/simple-icons.json` - [x] I optimized the icon with SVGO or SVGOMG - [x] The SVG `viewbox` is `0 0 24 24` ### Description - The color hex #873EFF is based on the official Woo brand guidelines (Woo Purple 40). Icon source: - Official SVG from Woo brand and logo guidelines: https://woocommerce.com/brand-and-logo-guidelines/ (from the "Download the logo" assets)
